body.title: [sprint] Purge goal-KR machinery from outcome, eval-run, weekly-retro skills
body.intention: I intend that outcome, eval-run, and weekly-retro no longer reference goal KRs or the palace.goals.* store — keeping the capability-snapshot and hypothesis-confirmation value while dropping the KR-tracking machinery that goals required.
body.description:
Three skills still assume active G-xxx goals with KR tracking. Per Akien (2026-06-26): KRs are dropped/folded, not re-pointed — the weekly retro wasn't happening; the intention+proof-on-close already handles the provable/disprovable requirement that KRs tried to serve. Each skill has a valuable core to keep:
- outcome: keep hypothesis verdict (confirmed/falsified/needs-more-time); DROP Step 5 'Update goal KR progress' and the 'KR impact' field from the verdict template.
- eval-run: keep the 5 capability questions as a standalone snapshot; DROP the 'Map to goal KRs' step and the palace.goals.* SQL query.
- weekly-retro: keep hypothesis confirmation rate + what-changes-about-priorities synthesis; DROP the 'Pull goal KR snapshots' step, the goal KR trend question, and the '## Goal KR trends' output section. The retro becomes a compact hypothesis-review, no palace.goals.* dependency.

**Affected files:** skills/outcome/SKILL.md, skills/eval-run/SKILL.md, skills/weekly-retro/SKILL.md.
**Design rules:** delete-after-successful-merge, goals-being-retired, rip-out-over-deprecate-in-place.
**Scope boundary:** IN - remove goal-KR steps and fields from all three. Keep each skill's non-KR value. OUT - the weekly cadence scheduling (already handled in day-close step 13.5; don't touch that here). OUT - any palace.goals.* data (historical). OUT - other skill files.
**Test plan:** grep skills/outcome/SKILL.md skills/eval-run/SKILL.md skills/weekly-retro/SKILL.md for 'G-xxx', 'palace.goals', 'KR', 'goal KR' -> 0 matches. Read each to confirm the valuable core (hypothesis review, capability snapshot, priority synthesis) is intact and readable without the KR machinery.
**Completion criteria:** `grep -c 'palace\.goals\|G-xxx\|goal KR\|KR progress\|KR trend\|feeds goal' skills/outcome/SKILL.md skills/eval-run/SKILL.md skills/weekly-retro/SKILL.md` returns 0 total.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
